mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals imminent economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0, or the signal loses predictive power in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand contraction, reducing corporate revenue expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52% over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, boosting corporate profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during post-shock recovery periods do not correlate with positive 20-day forward returns, or the mechanism predictive accuracy drops below 50% in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price indices combined with artificially low policy rates create stagflationary expectations, forcing equity repricing and triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with a positive return frequency exceeding 60% in historical samples.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly increases in nonfarm payrolls reflect sustained labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings growth that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns, or the signal rank IC falls below 0.01 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth falls below 1.0 percent, aggregate demand weakness signals an elevated recession probability, prompting defensive portfolio shifts that produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in >65% of quarters where GDPC1 growth is below 1.0%, invalidating the recession-risk pricing mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Diverging industrial production and unemployment trends, where output contracts while joblessness rises, confirm a hard economic landing scenario that triggers immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The co-occurrence of falling INDPRO and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 51% over a 10-year backtest.
